items.q: How are phobias MAINTAINED according to Mowrer's two-process model?
items.correct: Through operant conditioning - avoidance reduces anxiety (negative reinforcement).
items.misconception: Through classical conditioning continuing over time.
items.why: Classical conditioning ACQUIRES the phobia; operant conditioning MAINTAINS it. Students conflate these. Avoiding the feared object reduces anxiety, reinforcing the avoidance.

items.correct: Learning by observing the consequences others receive for behaviour.
items.misconception: Being directly rewarded for copying someone.
items.why: Students confuse vicarious with direct reinforcement. 'Vicarious' means experienced through someone else - you see them rewarded/punished and adjust your own behaviour accordingly.

items.q: Which part of the psyche operates on the reality principle?
items.why: Students confuse ego (reality) with superego (morality). The ego mediates between id's demands and reality's constraints. The superego is the internalised moral conscience.
Correct answer: Feeling what someone else feels. “Disliking everyone” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Caring from outside. “Sharing the feeling fully” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Cognitive + affective = how many types of empathy listed?
items.why: Exact answer: 2. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
